content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Solving Debugging Bottlenecks for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to leverage SaaS starter kits with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship stable products faster as a freelancer.
content.answer_block: Debugging hell kills your profit margins and client trust. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you shift from manual troubleshooting to architectural oversight. Use Antigravity to scaffold your logic and Claude Code to perform autonomous, context-aware debugging, effectively turning your dev environment into a self-healing production pipeline.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal at 2 AM, the production build is failing, and the client deadline is breathing down your neck. The code you wrote three hours ago has become an impenetrable labyrinth of bugs. This is the silent killer of the freelance career: the inability to debug efficiently, which turns high-value projects into low-margin nightmares.

But the best in the business don't spend their lives hunting semicolons. They use battle-tested SaaS starter kits as their foundation. By standardizing your stack, you eliminate the 'unknowns' that cause 90% of bugs, allowing you to focus on shipping features rather than fixing syntax errors. It is time to stop coding from scratch and start building with intent.

steps.title: Architecting the Core
steps.description: Initialize a modular SaaS starter kit that separates your business logic from the boilerplate, ensuring that bugs are contained within specific modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a modular SaaS architecture with TypeScript, Supabase auth, and Stripe integration, ensuring strict separation of concerns for easier debugging.

steps.title: Automated Linting and Type Checking
steps.description: Enforce strict typing to catch errors before they ever hit the runtime environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure ESLint and TypeScript strict mode to identify potential runtime errors, specifically targeting null-pointer exceptions and type mismatches.

steps.title: Context-Aware Debugging with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to analyze the stack trace and trace the execution flow through your starter kit's standardized directory structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current error log and correlate it with the recent file changes in the /services directory to pinpoint the regression.

steps.title: Regression Testing Implementation
steps.description: Once a bug is fixed, write a test case within the starter kit framework to ensure it never returns, protecting your client's long-term investment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vitest suite for the current module that covers the edge cases discovered during the recent debugging session.

steps.title: Optimizing Performance Loops
steps.description: Finalize your build by using AI to refactor inefficient code paths that often hide subtle, intermittent bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for performance bottlenecks in the data fetching layer and refactor for O(1) or O(log n) efficiency.

pro_tips: Always store your 'Known Good State' as a git tag before running AI-assisted refactors.
Use Claude Code's --dry-run flag to inspect AI-suggested patches before applying them to your production branch.
Standardize your error logging format across all projects so your AI tools can parse them instantly.
Treat your starter kit as a product; update it every time you solve a complex bug to improve your future velocity.
Disable auto-save when using AI agents to prevent half-finished code from triggering automated deployment pipelines.
content.next_steps: Once you have stabilized your starter kit, begin building a library of reusable components that you can deploy across all your client projects to maximize your hourly rate.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start scaling your freelance business with a proven, AI-assisted workflow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
